summary.brief: Rosenzweig v. Hadpawat. Medical malpractice case involving cardiology.

evidence_index.text: On September 8, 2014, the plaintiff's wife (hereinafter the decedent) presented to an urgent care clinic at the defendant CityMD with complaints of intermittent joint pain in her upper extremities for three days.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The decedent reported a history of hypertension and Hashimoto's disease for which she was taking medication.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: On September 11, 2014, the decedent visited her long-time physician, the defendant Michael S. Richheimer, an allergist and internist, at the office of the defendant Prohealth Care Associates, LLP.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Within hours of her visit to Richheimer, the decedent suffered a fatal cardiopulmonary arrest.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: An autopsy revealed that her cause of death was '[a]rteriosclerotic heart disease with thrombosis of [the] right coronary artery and myocardial infarction.'
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: ORDERED that the order is reversed insofar as appealed from, on the law, with one bill of costs payable by the defendants appearing separately and filing separate briefs.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

evidence_index.text: In opposition to the defendants' prima facie showing on the element of departure, the plaintiff raised triable issues of fact by submitting the redacted affirmation of an expert, who opined in a nonconclusory fashion that the defendants departed from the standard of care by failing to properly conduct a differential diagnosis and perform an echocardiogram.
evidence_index.speaker: Plaintiff's expert

evidence_index.text: Here, the expert, who was in the practice of internal medicine and primary care for more than 35 years, demonstrated based on experience and knowledge that he or she was qualified to render an opinion regarding the symptomology and diagnosis of heart disease.
evidence_index.speaker: Court reasoning

evidence_index.text: Accordingly, the Supreme Court should have denied those branches of the defendants' separate motions which were for summary judgment dismissing the causes of action alleging medical malpractice and wrongful death.
evidence_index.speaker: Judge's conclusion

initial_state.chief_complaint: Intermittent joint pain in upper extremities for three days
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Decedent reported not feeling well for two weeks, hot flashes, and weakness and intermittent burning sensation in her arms that awakened her at night.

requestables.name: Blood tests
requestables.description: Blood tests ordered by Dr. Richheimer

timeline_phases.name: Initial Presentation
timeline_phases.description: Decedent presents to urgent care and later to her long-time physician

key_events.event: Presentation to urgent care with joint pain
Visit to long-time physician with additional symptoms

decision_points.clinical_context: Decedent presented with symptoms suggestive of cardiac issues
decision_points.prompt: What diagnostic test should be performed to evaluate cardiac function?

options.clinical_reasoning: Echocardiogram is standard for evaluating cardiac function and ruling out heart disease.
Blood tests alone may not adequately assess cardiac function.

expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert evidence established echocardiogram is standard for cardiac evaluation

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to perform echocardiogram missed critical cardiac diagnosis
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Echocardiogram would have identified cardiac issues

patient_outcome.description: Decedent suffered a fatal cardiopulmonary arrest

key_findings: Breach of duty in failing to perform echocardiogram
Causation established - failure to diagnose cardiac condition led to death

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Echocardiogram would have shown cardiac issues

factual_timeline.event: Decedent presents to urgent care with joint pain
Decedent visits long-time physician with additional symptoms
